task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Scan the EAN-13 barcode using bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam interface displays a successfully scanned 13-digit EAN number in its result/output area.

task_completion.description: Query the inventory.db database for the scanned EAN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A database client (e.g., DB Browser for SQLite) or terminal shows a SELECT query being executed on C:\workspace\data\inventory.db using the 13-digit EAN scanned in the previous step.

task_completion.description: Create the audit_report.txt file with the product details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file C:\workspace\audit_report.txt containing the exact format: 'EAN: [number]', 'Name: [name]', 'Brand: [brand]'.

task_completion.description: Execute an UPDATE statement to mark the stock as verified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A database client or terminal shows an UPDATE SQL command executed on inventory.db setting 'stock_verified = 1' for the specific scanned EAN.

integrity.description: The agent used the bcWebCam GUI to scan the barcode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam GUI is visible and shows the scanned result, proving the agent didn't bypass the required software by reading the barcode directly via a script or VLM vision.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual SQLite database.
integrity.visual_evidence: SQL queries (SELECT and UPDATE) are visible in a terminal or database client interacting with inventory.db, rather than the agent fabricating the data or mocking the output.

integrity.description: The audit report contains genuine extracted data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The product Name and Brand in the text file match the results returned from the database query shown in earlier screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output report path is C:\workspace\audit_report.txt. The database path is C:\workspace\data\inventory.db.
